knownFor: Accor ibis reliability, Vikhroli's most consistent budget option, great beds at a budget price, 24-hr Spice It restaurant, and easy eastern highway access
address: Godrej One Campus, Pirojshanagar, Vikhroli East, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400079

name: Fairfield by Marriott Mumbai International Airport
tagline: Marriott's smart-value airport brand — everything you need, nothing you don't, and breakfast done right.
description: The Fairfield by Marriott Mumbai International Airport is the brand's strongest value argument: a proper four-star hotel with full Marriott standards — excellent beds, reliable WiFi, a well-maintained gym, a decent pool — positioned near the airport at a price that undercuts every comparable option. The breakfast buffet is a particular highlight, consistently praised in reviews as a genuine hotel-standard spread rather than the token offering many transit hotels provide. The location near the Sahar international airport zone makes it practical for early departures and late arrivals. For travellers who want the Marriott name and standards at a fraction of the JW or Renaissance rate, Fairfield delivers the best case for its tier.
knownFor: Marriott standards at a fair price near the airport, outstanding breakfast buffet, reliable beds and service, and the most logical mid-range airport stay in Mumbai
address: Sahar Airport Road, Andheri East, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400099

name: Holiday Inn Mumbai International Airport
tagline: IHG's glass-lobby airport four-star — a practical and reliable international transit option near T2.
description: The Holiday Inn Mumbai International Airport is a solid, full-service four-star hotel positioned near the international terminal — a glass-walled lobby property that delivers the IHG brand's consistent standard without the complexity of a luxury stay. The rooms are well-sized, the pool and fitness facilities are properly maintained, and the restaurant serves international food around the clock. The location makes early morning check-outs and late-night check-ins straightforward. The hotel is particularly popular with flight crew, international business travellers in transit, and families who need a comfortable, no-surprises stay near the airport.
knownFor: IHG four-star consistency near T2, glass-lobby design, 24-hr food service, airport-convenient check-in/check-out, and flight crew standard cleanliness
address: Sahar Airport Road, Andheri East, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400099

name: The Suba International Hotel
tagline: Andheri's iPod-room boutique — 72 tech-forward rooms near the airport with a Bollywood nightlife address.
description: The Suba International Hotel near Mumbai's international airport positions itself as a tech-forward boutique property — most notably through iPod-controlled room systems that allow guests to manage lighting, temperature, and entertainment through a bedside iPad dock. The 72 rooms are designed with a contemporary aesthetic and equipped with minifridges, personalised stationery, and complimentary mineral water. The location off Sahar Road places guests near both airport terminals and within reach of Andheri's bar and nightlife corridor, while the Infinity Bar on the premises draws a local following. For business travellers who want more personality than a standard airport transit hotel and less formality than a five-star, Suba International fills the gap.
knownFor: iPod-controlled room systems, tech-forward boutique design near the airport, Infinity Bar, and 72 well-equipped rooms at an intelligent price point
address: Sahar Road, Opposite Cigarette Factory, Andheri East, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400099

name: Hotel Regal Enclave
tagline: Khar's dependable four-star — midway between Bandra and Juhu, with a pool and the suburb's quiet residential energy.
description: Hotel Regal Enclave in Khar West is a four-star hotel that occupies the useful middle ground between Bandra's intensity and Juhu's beachside chaos — a quiet, residential neighbourhood with easy access to both. The hotel has a pool (rare in this area at this price), a reliable restaurant, and well-maintained rooms with the standard four-star amenities. Its location on the Linking Road-adjacent lanes of Khar means shopping, the Bandra social scene, and Juhu Beach are all accessible within 15 minutes. For families on longer Mumbai stays, or business travellers whose work takes them to the BKC corridor, the Regal Enclave's combination of suburban calm and central access is genuinely useful.
knownFor: Pool in quiet Khar West, midpoint between Bandra and Juhu, residential neighbourhood energy, and one of the few four-stars in this stretch with outdoor swimming
address: 43, Linking Road, Khar West, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400052

name: Hotel Tunga International
tagline: Andheri's three-star seafood gem — 24 modern rooms, complimentary breakfast, and a kitchen famous for its tiger prawns.
description: The International by Tunga in Andheri is a modest three-star property that punches well above its size thanks to a kitchen that takes seafood seriously. The in-house restaurant is known for its tiger prawns, lobster, and coastal Indian preparations — a legacy that draws both hotel guests and local office workers for lunch and dinner. The 24 rooms are modern, clean, and equipped with high-speed WiFi, complimentary continental breakfast, and mineral water. The Andheri location — convenient for the airport, SEEPZ, the western suburbs, and both Juhu and Bandra — makes it a practical base for domestic business travellers who want something better than a transit corridor hotel but don't need a five-star lobby.
knownFor: In-house seafood restaurant with tiger prawns and lobster, 24 well-equipped rooms, complimentary breakfast, and Andheri's most talked-about hotel kitchen
address: MIDC Road, Andheri East, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400093

name: Nap Manor Hostels
tagline: The family-friendly hostel that took Mumbai by surprise — clean, safe, and genuinely good for groups and solo alike.
description: Nap Manor Hostels is one of Mumbai's most consistently well-reviewed hostel properties — a rare example of a hostel that is equally suitable for solo backpackers, couples, and families with children. The design is clean and contemporary without being sterile; the common spaces are genuinely comfortable; and the safety and hygiene standards consistently draw mentions in reviews from guests who have stayed at hostels across Asia. The property operates near the airport, making it an excellent first or last night option for international arrivals and departures. Dorm options range from 4-bed female-only to mixed, and private rooms are available for those who need privacy. The staff are well-trained and responsive.
knownFor: Family-friendly hostel with genuine safety standards, clean contemporary design, airport proximity, female-only dorms, and the most positively reviewed hostel for solo female travellers in Mumbai
address: Andheri East, Near Mumbai International Airport, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400059

name: Moxy Mumbai Andheri West
tagline: Marriott's playful brand lands in Andheri West — rooftop bar, pool, DJ nights, and a vibe that suits the neighbourhood.
description: Moxy Mumbai Andheri West is the most fun four-star hotel in the northern suburbs — a Marriott Bonvoy property that refuses to take itself too seriously, in the best possible way. The 105 rooms are compact but brilliantly designed: motion-activated LED guidelights, walk-in showers, vintage Moxy telephones with USB ports, and smart wall features that double as storage and hanging space. The rooftop Moxy Sky lounge — a rooftop bar and Asian lounge with weekend DJ sets, skyline views, and creative cocktails — draws a local crowd alongside hotel guests. Moxy Kitchen on the ground floor serves small plates and spirited cocktails in a lively diner setting. The outdoor pool and well-equipped gym are genuinely good. The location in Oshiwara, Andheri West, places guests minutes from Versova Beach, Fun Republic Mall, ISKCON temple, Prithvi Theatre, and the best of Juhu — and just 9 km from the international airport. For younger travellers, Bollywood-adjacent visitors, and anyone who wants Marriott standards at a livelier, more affordable price, this is the sharpest mid-range choice in the suburbs.
knownFor: Moxy Sky rooftop bar with weekend DJs, compact smartly-designed rooms, outdoor pool, Andheri West location near Versova and Prithvi Theatre, and Marriott Bonvoy points
address: Plot No. B-38, CTS No. 711, Oshiwara, Andheri West, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400053

name: Chateau Windsor Hotel
tagline: 50 metres from Marine Drive — a balcony, a sea breeze, and a rooftop terrace that earns its own fan following.
description: Chateau Windsor Hotel is one of the best-positioned three-star hotels in all of Mumbai — a property on Veer Nariman Road, literally 50 metres from Marine Drive's iconic seafront promenade, with sea-view balcony rooms that offer the Queen's Necklace experience at a fraction of the five-star price. The hotel is over 80 years old and the vintage interiors reflect that age honestly — some rooms have the worn edges of a well-used heritage building, others have been renovated to a cleaner standard. What earns repeat visits is the rooftop terrace, where Chateau Windsor serves breakfast and light snacks with panoramic views of the sea; K. Rustom's legendary ice cream parlour is 200 metres away; Wankhede Cricket Stadium is 650 metres; Churchgate railway station is a short walk. The staff are consistently praised as friendly and helpful, and the in-house shared kitchen is available for guests who want to self-cater. For the budget-conscious traveller who insists on a South Mumbai location, this is a genuinely excellent choice.
knownFor: 50 metres from Marine Drive, sea-view balcony rooms, rooftop terrace breakfast, heritage building character, and the best budget location on the Marine Drive stretch
address: 86, Veer Nariman Road, Churchgate, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400020

name: Hotel Sahil
tagline: A quiet and clean three-star in central South Mumbai — Mahalaxmi adjacent, sensibly priced, and reliably comfortable.
description: Hotel Sahil in the Kemps Corner–August Kranti Marg belt is a well-regarded three-star that serves as a calm, practical base for travellers who want the South Mumbai experience without Colaba's tourist concentration or Nariman Point's corporate pricing. The rooms are clean, properly air-conditioned, and comfortable. The Rivaz restaurant on the premises serves halal non-vegetarian dishes as well as a vegetarian menu. The hotel is conveniently placed between Mahalaxmi, Breach Candy, and Kemps Corner — areas with good restaurants, local transport, and a quieter, more residential quality that budget five-stars lack. Staff are consistently described in reviews as friendly, attentive, and genuinely helpful with city logistics.
knownFor: Quiet South Mumbai three-star, Rivaz restaurant with halal options, Kemps Corner location, clean and reliable, and excellent value in an expensive neighbourhood
address: Plot No. 26/28, C-Road, August Kranti Marg, Kemps Corner, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400036

name: Hotel Heritage (Comfort Inn Heritage)
tagline: Byculla's heritage hotel next to the zoo and the Gloria Church — a clean, affordable base for central Mumbai.
description: Hotel Heritage (formerly Comfort Inn Heritage) sits on Sant Savta Marg in Byculla, opposite the Gloria Church — one of Mumbai's oldest Portuguese-era buildings — and a short walk from the Byculla railway station and the historic Rani Baug (the Jijamata Udyan zoo and botanical garden). The hotel occupies a position at the geographic heart of Mumbai that gives unusually good access to both South Mumbai (CST, Kala Ghoda, Colaba are 20 minutes away) and Central Mumbai (Dadar, Matunga, Parel are 15 minutes). Rooms are clean, air-conditioned, and maintained to a consistent standard. The breakfast is praised in reviews as genuinely good. The staff are warm and welcoming. For travellers who want central Mumbai access at a fair price without South Mumbai's premium, this is one of the more thoughtful choices in the mid-range.
knownFor: Central Byculla location — walkable to the station, Gloria Church, and Rani Baug zoo, warm hospitality, good breakfast, and the fairest rate for central Mumbai access
address: Sant Savta Marg, Opposite Gloria Church, Byculla, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400027

name: Hotel Karl Residency
tagline: Andheri West's pool hotel — a calm spot near the airport with a poolside bar, marble rooms, and Lallubhai Park next door.
description: Hotel Karl Residency in Andheri West is a Parsi-run three-star hotel that occupies a surprisingly serene setting adjacent to Lallubhai Park — a genuine pocket of greenery in the middle of busy Andheri. The hotel's outdoor pool with poolside bar is its signature feature and differentiates it from every other hotel in this price range in the neighbourhood. The rooms are furnished with marble and granite and are well air-conditioned. Andheri railway station is a 10-minute walk; the international airport is 4 km away; Juhu Beach is 2.3 km. The Saffron Spring restaurant serves Indian cuisine, and the buffet breakfast is included in most rates. For domestic leisure travellers with families, or business visitors who want a pool and the airport within reach at a budget rate, the Karl Residency fills a specific and useful need.
knownFor: Outdoor pool with poolside bar near Lallubhai Park, marble and granite rooms, 4 km from airport, included breakfast, and one of the few poolside budget options in Andheri
address: 36, Lallubhai Park Road, Near Lallubhai Park, Andheri West, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400058

name: Citizen Hotel
tagline: Juhu Beach on your doorstep — steps down to the sand, Bay View restaurant, and the most affordable beachfront position in Mumbai.
description: Citizen Hotel stands directly on Juhu Tara Road with the beach literally at the bottom of its garden stairs — a position that five-star hotels in the same neighbourhood charge a five-star premium for. At three-star pricing, this makes Citizen one of the most unusual value propositions in Mumbai: 45 rooms, all with sea-view potential, a Bay View restaurant and terrace bar overlooking the beach (the ideal spot for an evening cocktail watching Juhu's sunset theatre), complimentary breakfast, and Prithvi Theatre 1.2 km away. The rooms are small — reviews consistently mention this — and service quality varies, but for travellers whose priority is the Juhu Beach experience at an accessible price, nothing else in this category compares. The hotel has maintained its position on the sand since the 1990s and retains a loyal base of repeat visitors.
knownFor: Direct beach access on Juhu Tara Road, Bay View restaurant overlooking the sand, complimentary breakfast, most affordable beachfront stay in Mumbai, and Prithvi Theatre proximity
address: 960, Juhu Tara Road, Juhu Beach, Juhu, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400049

name: Click Hotel Andheri MIDC
tagline: A clean, honest three-star in Andheri MIDC — free breakfast, airport 4.8 km, and everything a business traveller needs.
description: Click Hotel Andheri MIDC is a straightforward, well-run three-star in the MIDC industrial zone of Andheri East — the kind of hotel that consistently positive reviews are built on: clean rooms, reliable WiFi, free breakfast, free parking, courteous staff, and an airport (4.8 km) that makes early check-outs easy. The rooms are air-conditioned and equipped with cable TV and en-suite bathrooms. Room service and a doctor-on-call are available. The location puts guests near Andheri station (3.8 km), SEEPZ, and the eastern express highway. For domestic business travellers visiting MIDC-area companies, or budget-conscious international visitors whose itinerary centres on the northern suburbs, Click Hotel MIDC delivers exactly what is advertised, competently and honestly.
knownFor: Free breakfast and free parking, 4.8 km from airport, clean and reliable, Andheri MIDC's most consistently reviewed honest three-star, and doctor on call
address: MIDC, Andheri East, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400093

name: Residency Hotel Dadar
tagline: Dadar's most dependable mid-ranger — central Mumbai's busiest rail junction, Shivaji Park, and Aaswad's misal all within walking distance.
description: Residency Hotel in Dadar occupies a genuinely useful position: walking distance from Dadar railway station (one of Mumbai's most important transit nodes, connecting western and central lines as well as long-distance trains), Shivaji Park (the city's most politically charged public ground), and Aaswad — the restaurant that won the world's best vegetarian food title. This makes it one of the best-positioned hotels for travellers whose itinerary includes both South Mumbai (20 minutes by train) and the suburbs (20 minutes by rail to Bandra or Andheri). The rooms are clean, air-conditioned, and functional. The hotel restaurant covers basic Indian food. At this price point and with this location, Residency Dadar regularly outperforms properties twice its rate.
knownFor: Dadar railway hub proximity (western and central rail lines), walking distance to Aaswad misal and Shivaji Park, and the most transit-connected mid-range hotel in central Mumbai
address: S.K. Bole Road, Dadar West, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400028

name: Hotel Midtown Pritam
tagline: Dadar East's family-friendly three-star — close to the station, near Chaitanya seafood, and well priced for longer stays.
description: Hotel Midtown Pritam is a well-regarded three-star in Dadar East that caters primarily to domestic families, business travellers, and visitors transiting through Mumbai who want a clean, no-fuss stay near the central railway junction. The hotel has maintained its standards for many years and earns consistent positive reviews for its clean rooms, helpful staff, and practical amenities. The location in Dadar East puts guests near the station, the nearby Chaitanya Malvani seafood restaurant (one of the most beloved budget seafood options in the city), and the wholesale flower and produce markets that are a feature of Dadar's morning energy. The proximity to both CST and domestic railway platforms makes it a practical choice for train arrivals.
knownFor: Dadar East railway proximity, family-friendly three-star consistency, near Chaitanya seafood restaurant, and a reliable choice for domestic train arrivals with good value rates
address: Pritam Estate, Dadar East, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400014

name: Ginger Hotel Mumbai Airport
tagline: The Tata Group's budget answer to the airport — clean, honest, affordable, and 2 km from Terminal 1.
description: Ginger Hotel Mumbai Airport (near Santacruz domestic terminal) is the most practical budget stay for early-morning domestic departures and late-night arrivals in Mumbai. As a Tata Group property, the Ginger brand guarantees a baseline of cleanliness, safety, and reliability that smaller independent hotels cannot consistently match. The rooms are compact but thoughtfully designed — good beds, powerful showers, reliable WiFi — and the 24-hour checkout policy (guests check out 24 hours after checking in, not at a fixed time) is a genuine practical advantage for travellers on unpredictable schedules. The in-house restaurant covers meals round the clock. At under ₹3,500 per night, it offers the Tata name and standard at the most accessible price point in Mumbai.
knownFor: Tata Group reliability at the lowest price near the airport, 24-hr checkout policy, 2 km from domestic Terminal 1, clean rooms, and the most accessible Ginger in Mumbai
address: Andheri-Kurla Road, Santacruz East, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400029

name: Hotel A.K. International
tagline: Fort's budget business hotel — CSMT a 5-minute walk, and some of the best value rates in South Mumbai.
description: Hotel A.K. International in Fort is a budget three-star that earns its steady stream of repeat bookings through one principal advantage: location. The hotel is a 5-minute walk from CSMT (Chhatrapati Shivaji Terminus), one of Mumbai's main rail hubs and a UNESCO World Heritage Site in its own right, and within easy walking distance of the entire Fort commercial district, Kala Ghoda's galleries and restaurants, and the CST ferry terminal for Elephanta Caves. The rooms are straightforward — clean, air-conditioned, with reliable WiFi and basic amenities — and the rates represent some of the best value available for a South Mumbai position. For travellers arriving by train or prioritising the Fort heritage corridor, this is a logical, unpretentious choice.
knownFor: 5-minute walk to CSMT, South Mumbai Fort location, one of the lowest rates in the area, clean and practical, and the most convenient hotel for rail arrivals to Mumbai
address: Near CSMT, Fort, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400001

name: Hotel Regent
tagline: Andheri East's 44-room mid-ranger — business-friendly, airport-convenient, and consistently reliable.
description: Hotel Regent in Andheri East is a 44-room three-star that has built a solid reputation among domestic business travellers for the fundamentals that matter most in this category: clean, air-conditioned rooms; a reliable business centre; security safes in every room; fast WiFi; and a front desk that operates properly around the clock. The location is close to the SEEPZ, MIDC, and Andheri's commercial clusters, and the airport is within easy reach. For travellers who arrive late, leave early, and need a competent, uncomplicated base in Andheri East — rather than a hotel that tries too hard or charges too much — Regent delivers without drama.
knownFor: 44 well-maintained rooms in Andheri East, security safes, business centre, consistent quality for the price, and reliable airport-corridor convenience
address: Andheri East, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400069

name: The Unicontinental Hotel
tagline: Khar West's lively three-star — pub, café, two restaurants, and Linking Road shopping on the doorstep.
description: The Unicontinental Hotel in Khar West is a popular mid-range property that earns its following by being genuinely lively and well-located rather than just adequate. The hotel has a pub, a café, and two full restaurants — an unusually complete food-and-drink offering for a property at this price point — and the Linking Road shopping strip runs right past the front door. The rooms are air-conditioned and equipped with standard modern amenities. Bandra's nightlife and social scene are accessible within minutes; the airport is around 12 km; Juhu Beach is nearby. For young domestic travellers, smaller group travel, or anyone who wants the best of the Khar-Bandra corridor at a budget rate with the bonus of an on-site pub, this is a reliably popular choice.
knownFor: On-site pub, café, and two restaurants, Linking Road shopping access, lively Khar West location close to Bandra, and one of the better F&B packages at this price tier
address: Linking Road, Khar West, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400052

name: Hotel Emerald
tagline: Juhu's four-star apartment-hotel — upscale rooms, serviced apartments, and 5 minutes on foot to the beach.
description: The Emerald in Juhu is a four-star hotel that offers both hotel rooms and serviced apartment-style accommodation — a combination that makes it particularly popular with extended-stay guests and families. The serviced apartments come with full kitchens and living areas; the hotel rooms are upscale and well-appointed. The property is a 5-minute walk from Juhu Beach, and the neighbourhood surroundings — Prithvi Theatre, ISKCON temple, and the restaurant-lined Juhu Tara Road — make it one of the more culturally rich locations for a mid-range stay in the northern suburbs. The hotel has a pool and gym, and the service is described in reviews as attentive and professional. For domestic business visitors or families on longer Mumbai stays who want Juhu Beach access without the JW Marriott rate, Emerald is the most comfortable option.
knownFor: Juhu Beach 5-minute walk, serviced apartments with full kitchens for extended stays, four-star amenities at a reasonable rate, and central Juhu neighbourhood access
address: Palm Grove, Juhu Tara Road, Juhu, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400049

name: Hotel Marigold Residency
tagline: Andheri's compact three-star next to the airport — clean rooms, friendly staff, and the most transparent pricing near the terminals.
description: Marigold Residency sits in the Marol area of Andheri East, directly next to Sangeet Plaza and the Fire Brigade junction — a position that makes it one of the most honest airport-adjacent budget choices in Mumbai. The hotel is small (around 30 rooms) and straightforward: clean air-conditioned rooms, complimentary mineral water, WiFi, and a front desk that is helpful without overpromising. There is no pool and no restaurant of note — guests eat at the surrounding MIDC eateries and Marol's modest food scene — but the hotel is reliably clean, the staff are consistently praised for being cooperative and courteous, and the proximity to the airport (under 2 km to both terminals via the internal roads) makes it the most practical no-fuss option for budget airport stays.
knownFor: Under 2 km to both airport terminals, small and clean 30-room property, transparent pricing, cooperative staff, and the most honest budget airport stay in Mumbai
address: Next to Sangeet Plaza, Marol Fire Brigade, Marol, Andheri East, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400059

name: Hotel Suba Galaxy
tagline: Andheri West's most value-conscious four-star — rooftop pool, good restaurant, and BKC within striking distance.
description: Hotel Suba Galaxy is a four-star property in Andheri West that fills the gap between Andheri's business-corridor three-stars and the five-star properties near the airport. The hotel has a rooftop pool, a gym, and a well-regarded multi-cuisine restaurant (Gazebo) that serves the hotel's mostly corporate clientele along with walk-in local diners. The rooms are well-sized, modern, and properly equipped. The Andheri West location gives reasonable access to Juhu Beach (3 km), Versova (4 km), BKC (8 km), and the airport (5 km). The hotel frequently appears on shortlists for domestic corporate travel because it reliably delivers a four-star experience at a rate meaningfully below the Marriott and IHG properties in the same corridor.
knownFor: Rooftop pool at a four-star price point, Gazebo restaurant, good corporate value, Andheri West base between Juhu and the airport, and consistent standards for repeat business visitors
address: Andheri West, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400053

name: Hotel Lords Inn Fort
tagline: Free parking in Fort, 8 minutes to CSMT — a practical, clean three-star in the city's most historically rich corridor.
description: Hotel Lords Fort is a three-star property in the Fort district of South Mumbai that earns its place on this list through the combination of free private parking (a genuine rarity in Fort), a location that is 8 minutes on foot to CSMT, and rooms that are clean, air-conditioned, and priced honestly. Guests who arrive in Mumbai by road with a car, or who rent one for a Mumbai trip, will find the parking advantage almost worth the booking on its own in a part of the city where parking is expensive and scarce. The hotel is also conveniently placed for the CSMVS museum, Kala Ghoda, the Chor Bazaar, and the Ballard Estate heritage walk. The restaurant covers basic multi-cuisine meals.
knownFor: Free private parking in Fort (extremely rare at this price), 8 minutes to CSMT, heritage Fort location, clean and honest three-star standards, and easy access to South Mumbai attractions
address: Fort, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400001

name: Pride Hotel Mumbai
tagline: Andheri's Pride Group four-star — pool, multi-cuisine dining, and solid business infrastructure at a sensible rate.
description: Pride Hotel Mumbai in Andheri is part of the Pride Hotels group — an Indian chain with a reputation for consistent quality and good value across its portfolio. The Mumbai property delivers a proper four-star product: a swimming pool, a well-equipped gym, multi-cuisine dining at the Utsav restaurant, a full business centre, and rooms that are spacious, clean, and properly maintained. The Andheri West location works for airport access (7 km), BKC meetings (12 km), and the general western suburbs itinerary. The hotel is particularly popular with domestic corporate groups and family leisure travel — the Pride Group's reputation for cleanliness and value has been built over many years across India, and the Mumbai property upholds it.
knownFor: Pride Group consistent four-star standards, swimming pool and gym, Utsav multi-cuisine restaurant, good corporate rates, and reliable quality for domestic business and family travel
address: Andheri West, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400058

name: The Leela Palace New Delhi
tagline: Delhi's most palatial address — Chanakyapuri's crown jewel, where diplomats and royalty feel at home.
description: The Leela Palace New Delhi is, by almost every measure, the most extravagant hotel in the capital. Situated in Chanakyapuri — the city's diplomatic enclave, where every major embassy, the Prime Minister's residence, and Rashtrapati Bhavan converge — the hotel was built to feel like a palace, and it succeeds completely. The architecture draws from traditional Indian palace design: the soaring atrium lobby with its hand-painted ceiling, the double staircases, the enormous chandeliers, and the liberal deployment of Italian marble throughout. The rooftop infinity pool is one of the defining luxury hotel images in India — a sheet of water that seems to extend to the treeline of Delhi's most serene diplomatic neighbourhood. The hotel's restaurant Megu has long been considered the finest Japanese restaurant in Delhi, while Jamavar brings serious South Indian coastal cuisine to the capital. The spa, Le Spa Leela, is among the best in India. Everything here — the rooms, the service, the F&B, the Sunday brunch, the location — operates at the highest level simultaneously.
knownFor: Rooftop infinity pool, Megu Japanese restaurant, Jamavar South Indian dining, Chanakyapuri diplomatic enclave address, and the most palatial public spaces in Delhi
address: Diplomatic Enclave, Chanakyapuri, New Delhi, Delhi 110023
amenities: 4 Restaurants & Bars (Megu, Jamavar, Le Cirque Signature, EICH)

name: The Oberoi New Delhi
tagline: Views of Humayun's Tomb from your bath — the purest expression of Oberoi luxury in the capital.
description: The Oberoi New Delhi is, in the opinion of many seasoned travellers and hospitality critics, the finest hotel in India — full stop. The property sits on Dr. Zakir Hussain Marg, and the positioning is extraordinary: every room looks out over either the Delhi Golf Club's 200-acre canopy of greens or Humayun's Tomb, the 16th-century Mughal mausoleum that presages the Taj Mahal and is perhaps the most beautiful monument in Delhi. Like the Mumbai Oberoi, the Delhi property deploys state-of-the-art air purification technology across the entire hotel — a genuinely meaningful feature in a city with significant air quality challenges. The 220 rooms and suites are among the most spacious of any five-star in Delhi. The hotel's rooftop bar Cirrus 9 is one of the great urban drinking perches in Asia — looking out over the golf course with the ridge behind it. The Oberoi Pâtisserie & Delicatessen in the lobby was the first of its kind in India. The three-Michelin-equivalent restaurant 361° brings together the best of Oberoi's culinary traditions. The butler programme is exceptional and the check-in process — entirely paperless, conducted in your room — sets the tone immediately.
knownFor: Views of Delhi Golf Club and Humayun's Tomb from every room, Cirrus 9 rooftop bar, Oberoi Pâtisserie, IoT air purification, and the most celebrated service culture in Indian hospitality
address: Dr. Zakir Hussain Marg, New Delhi, Delhi 110003
amenities: 3 Restaurants & Bars (361°, Cirrus 9 Rooftop Bar, Oberoi Pâtisserie)

name: ITC Maurya, a Luxury Collection Hotel
tagline: Home to Bukhara — consistently ranked one of the world's 50 best restaurants, in a hotel that has hosted every US President since Jimmy Carter.
description: ITC Maurya is Delhi's statesman hotel. Situated in the diplomatic enclave of Chanakyapuri, it has been the chosen residence of every visiting US President since Jimmy Carter, along with countless heads of state, prime ministers, and Nobel laureates — a guest list that tells you everything about its standing in the world's diplomatic community. The hotel is built in the grand ITC tradition: 437 rooms and suites, the Kaya Kalp Royal Spa (one of the most comprehensive in South Asia), and a roster of restaurants that no other Delhi hotel can match. Bukhara — the clay-oven restaurant serving North-West Frontier cuisine — has been consistently ranked among the top 50 restaurants in the world and the top 20 in Asia by the World's 50 Best list. It is, without question, the most famous Indian restaurant in India. Dum Pukht serves the royal Awadhi cuisine of Lucknow and has been recognised as one of the world's best classic restaurants by Condé Nast Traveler. Add Ottimo (Italian), Tian (Asian), and the Samaya tea lounge and you have a culinary universe under one roof. The ITC Towers wing offers butler service and an exclusive lounge. For those to whom food is the centrepiece of any hotel stay, there is no better address in Delhi.
knownFor: Bukhara restaurant (Top 50 in the world), Dum Pukht Awadhi cuisine, preferred hotel of visiting US Presidents, Kaya Kalp Spa, and ITC's signature eco-luxury philosophy
address: Diplomatic Enclave, Sardar Patel Marg, Chanakyapuri, New Delhi, Delhi 110021
amenities: 5 Restaurants & Bars (Bukhara, Dum Pukht, Ottimo, Tian, Samaya)

name: The Lodhi
tagline: The only hotel in Delhi where every room has a private plunge pool — a Leading Hotel of the World overlooking Humayun's Tomb.
description: The Lodhi makes a single claim that no other hotel in Delhi — or indeed most of India — can match: every one of its 96 rooms and suites features a private plunge pool set on a private balcony. The property spreads across seven acres on Lodi Road, directly opposite the Delhi Golf Course and a short walk from Lodhi Garden, the 15th-century parkland studded with Sayyid and Lodi dynasty tombs. The architecture, designed by Kerry Hill Architects, is a masterwork of contemporary design that manages to feel timeless in its use of natural materials — stone, water, and greenery dominate. The 50-metre heated outdoor lap pool is the longest in any Delhi hotel. The tennis and squash courts, Pilates studio, and world-class hammam spa together constitute the most complete wellness infrastructure in the capital. Indian Accent — widely considered the best restaurant in India and frequently in the global top 100 — operates from the Lodhi's grounds as an independent restaurant, though its culinary legacy is inextricably linked to the hotel. The Lodhi's concierges hold the coveted Les Clefs d'Or certification. The hotel is ranked among the top 25 in Asia. For the discerning guest who wants solitude, space, and private water at every category, this is the address.
knownFor: Private plunge pools in every room, 50-metre lap pool, Indian Accent restaurant (India's best), Hammam spa, Kerry Hill Architects design, and the most secluded luxury in central Delhi
address: Lodhi Road, New Delhi, Delhi 110003

name: Taj Mahal, New Delhi
tagline: The Taj's Delhi flagship — a Man Singh Road landmark with India's finest Chinese restaurant and old-world IHCL grandeur.
description: The Taj Mahal New Delhi occupies a privileged position on Man Singh Road — the broad, tree-lined avenue that connects India Gate to the Diplomatic Enclave — and has been the Taj Group's flagship Delhi property since the group first established itself here. The hotel is part of IHCL's collection of iconic city hotels and carries the full weight of the Taj's heritage service philosophy. The architecture is Raj-era colonial grandeur, refined to a contemporary gloss over decades. House of Ming — the hotel's Cantonese restaurant — is consistently rated the finest Chinese restaurant in Delhi, a city with no shortage of options. The Machan coffee shop is a beloved Delhi institution. The pool is one of the most gracious in the city, framed by lawns and landscaping. The Mansingh Road address puts the hotel within minutes of Khan Market, Lodi Garden, Pragati Maidan, and the full stretch of the Lutyens bungalow zone. The rooms and suites are classically Taj — deeply comfortable, generously sized, and impeccably maintained.
knownFor: House of Ming Chinese restaurant (Delhi's finest), Machan coffee shop, Man Singh Road location near Khan Market, Taj's legendary service, and colonial grandeur on Lutyens' Delhi's grandest avenue
address: 1, Man Singh Road, New Delhi, Delhi 110011
amenities: 4 Restaurants & Bars (House of Ming, Machan, Tea House, Ricks)

name: The Imperial, New Delhi
tagline: Built in 1931 — the grandest colonial hotel in Asia, on Janpath in the heart of Lutyens' New Delhi.
description: The Imperial New Delhi is not merely Delhi's oldest luxury hotel — it is one of the great colonial-era hotels of Asia, comparable in historical weight to Raffles Singapore or The Peninsula Hong Kong. Built in 1931 to the designs of D.J. Blomfield, with later contributions attributed to Edwin Lutyens and Herbert Baker, the hotel stands on Janpath at the ceremonial heart of Lutyens' planned capital, a short walk from Connaught Place and India Gate. The architecture is high Art Deco colonial: 12 acres of white colonnaded gardens, sweeping verandahs, mosaic tile floors, and corridors hung with one of the largest private art collections in India — over 5,000 works spanning three centuries of Indian history. The 234 rooms are enormous, with ceiling heights and proportions that no modern hotel can replicate. The gym spans 44,000 square feet and incorporates India's most elaborate hotel wellness complex. The hotel's restaurants — including 1911 (the fine dining room), Spice Route (South-East Asian), and the Atrium Bar — are destinations in their own right. The Sunday brunch at The Imperial is a Delhi institution that has been running for decades. This is the hotel that Delhi itself considers most its own.
knownFor: 1931 colonial architecture, 5,000-piece private art collection, 44,000-sq-ft wellness complex, Spice Route restaurant, Sunday brunch institution, and the most historically significant hotel in the capital
address: Janpath, New Delhi, Delhi 110001
amenities: 4 Restaurants & Bars (1911, Spice Route, Atrium Bar, The Patiala Peg Bar)

name: Taj Palace, New Delhi
tagline: Six acres of gardens in Chanakyapuri — the Taj Group's sprawling conference-and-leisure palace near the Diplomatic Enclave.
description: Taj Palace New Delhi sits on six acres of manicured grounds in Chanakyapuri, within the diplomatic enclave that also houses ITC Maurya and The Leela Palace. Where the Taj Mahal on Man Singh Road is the heritage flagship, Taj Palace is the grand resort-style property — sprawling, green, and built for the scale that large diplomatic and corporate events require. The hotel has 403 rooms and suites, multiple pools, extensive banquet and convention infrastructure, and the DLF Emporio and DLF Promenade luxury malls within walking distance. The Masala Art restaurant has long been one of Delhi's most beloved Indian fine-dining destinations, celebrated for its presentation of regional Indian cuisines. Orient Express — a restaurant designed to evoke the legendary train — is a Delhi original. The garden views from ground-floor rooms at Taj Palace are among the most peaceful experiences available at any Delhi five-star. This is the hotel for guests who want the Taj name with maximum space.
knownFor: Six-acre garden estate, Masala Art Indian fine dining, Orient Express restaurant, proximity to DLF Emporio mall, Diplomatic Enclave location, and the most spacious grounds of any Delhi five-star
address: 2, Sardar Patel Marg, Chanakyapuri, New Delhi, Delhi 110021
amenities: 5 Restaurants & Bars (Masala Art, Orient Express, Blue Ginger, Aqua, Tea Lounge)

name: Shangri-La Eros New Delhi
tagline: Connaught Place's most contemporary five-star — Shang Palace, a rooftop, and 100% plastic-free wellness in Lutyens' Delhi.
description: Shangri-La Eros New Delhi occupies a prime position in Connaught Place, placing it at the literal centre of Delhi — within walking distance of the Rajiv Chowk metro interchange, India Gate (1.5 km), Jantar Mantar, and the commercial and cultural spine of the capital. The hotel was fully refurbished in 2022 and the results are striking: a new Wellness Club with state-of-the-art equipment, advanced air purification, and a 100% plastic-free commitment throughout the property. Dining here is exceptional: Shang Palace serves Yunnan, Cantonese, and Sichuan cuisine and is the 39th Shang Palace globally (a brand that consistently earns Michelin stars across Asia). Sorrento holds the Golden Q certification from Ospitalità Italiana for authentic Italian excellence. Tamra is one of Delhi's most celebrated all-day dining venues with a 'world on a platter' buffet. Mister Chai has become the destination for upscale Indian street food. The Horizon Club executive lounge commands panoramic views of Lutyens Delhi. For a combination of central location, contemporary luxury, and dining depth, Shangri-La Eros is arguably the most complete five-star offering in the Connaught Place zone.
knownFor: Shang Palace Chinese restaurant (Michelin-pedigree brand), Tamra all-day dining, plastic-free wellness club, Horizon Club panoramic views, and the best-located five-star in central Delhi
address: 19, Ashoka Road, Connaught Place, New Delhi, Delhi 110001
amenities: 5 Restaurants & Bars (Shang Palace, Sorrento, Tamra, Mister Chai, Grappa)

name: Hyatt Regency New Delhi
tagline: R.K. Puram's Hyatt landmark — a foodie's five-star with TK's and La Piazza, near the Ring Road and South Delhi.
description: The Hyatt Regency New Delhi has long been known as the most food-forward of Delhi's five-stars — a hotel where the dining programme genuinely drives bookings, not just the rooms. TK's Oriental Grill is the go-to for teppanyaki in Delhi, drawing dedicated regulars from across the city. La Piazza is considered one of the better Italian restaurants in the capital. The multilevel Sunday brunch at the hotel is among the city's most popular and has been running for over two decades. The hotel sits in R.K. Puram, close to the Ring Road, making it equally convenient for South Delhi neighbourhoods like Vasant Vihar, Saket, and Mehrauli, as well as the Ministry corridor. The 517 rooms are generously proportioned and the club floors offer some of the best executive lounge experiences in Delhi. For corporate travellers who want a large-format Hyatt product at a marginally more accessible price point than the top-tier properties, this is a reliable and well-established choice.
knownFor: TK's Oriental Grill teppanyaki, La Piazza Italian restaurant, legendary Sunday brunch, well-connected R.K. Puram location, and the most food-driven luxury hotel experience in Delhi
address: Bhikaji Cama Place, Ring Road, New Delhi, Delhi 110066
amenities: 5 Restaurants & Bars (TK's Oriental Grill, La Piazza, Café, Polo Lounge, Sidewalk)

name: JW Marriott Hotel New Delhi Aerocity
tagline: The airport's finest address — JW's Delhi property puts five-star luxury 10 minutes from IGI Terminal 3.
description: The JW Marriott New Delhi Aerocity is the undisputed finest hotel in the Aerocity hospitality district — the purpose-built hotel corridor that flanks Indira Gandhi International Airport's Terminal 3. For guests with early-morning or late-night international flights, or those using Delhi as a hub city rather than a destination, this JW property removes every friction from the travel experience. The hotel has 821 rooms — one of the largest room counts of any Delhi luxury property — alongside a full-sized spa, multiple pools, and a dining programme that punches well above the typical airport hotel standard. Akira Back — the celebrity chef's eponymous Korean-Japanese fusion restaurant — is considered one of the best restaurants in Aerocity and draws guests from across Delhi. The Spa by JW is spacious and well-equipped. The Marriott Bonvoy executive lounge is excellent. For guests who need five-star standards and zero airport logistics headache, this is the correct answer.
knownFor: 10-minute drive from IGI Terminal 3, Akira Back Korean-Japanese restaurant, 821-room scale, Spa by JW, and the best airport-adjacent luxury in India
address: Asset Area 4, Hospitality District, Aerocity, New Delhi, Delhi 110037
amenities: 4 Restaurants & Bars (Akira Back, Sear, Lobby Lounge, Pool Bar)

name: The Roseate New Delhi
tagline: Mahipalpur's hidden garden sanctuary — a boutique-scale five-star with an extraordinary wellness programme and India Gate 12 km away.
description: The Roseate New Delhi is one of Delhi's quiet discoveries — a luxury property that operates at five-star level without the bombast of the city's larger hotel flagships. Tucked behind landscaped gardens in Mahipalpur, near the airport but with none of the corporate airport-hotel aesthetic, the hotel is built around a philosophy of wellness and natural materials. The spa, Spa by Roseate, is among the most thoughtfully designed hotel spas in Delhi, drawing on Ayurvedic traditions as well as contemporary global treatments. The pools and gardens are meticulously maintained and create a genuine sense of retreat. The restaurants — Pebble Street (all-day dining) and Kiyan (Indian fine dining with an outdoor terrace) — are both well-regarded. The hotel is a member of the Small Luxury Hotels of the World, which underscores its positioning: this is a hotel for guests who value curation and calm over sheer scale. Access to Aerocity, South Delhi, and the airport is straightforward.
knownFor: Garden sanctuary design, Spa by Roseate Ayurvedic wellness, Kiyan Indian fine dining, Small Luxury Hotels of the World membership, and the most tranquil five-star stay near Delhi airport
address: Plot No. 11 & 12, Samalkha, NH 8, Mahipalpur, New Delhi, Delhi 110037

name: The Claridges New Delhi
tagline: Lutyens' Delhi's most graceful heritage hotel — 1950s colonial elegance on Aurangzeb Road, three acres of lawns and arcaded verandahs.
description: The Claridges, named after (though legally unrelated to) the famous London hotel, has been part of New Delhi's social fabric since it opened in 1956 on Aurangzeb Road in the heart of Lutyens' Delhi. The hotel occupies three acres of garden in the Diplomatic Quarter, and the building's low white colonial architecture — arcaded verandahs, high ceilings, mosaic floors — has an ease and elegance that many more expensive hotels cannot purchase. The 132 rooms are classically furnished with generous proportions and the warm, slightly formal hospitality that defines old-school Delhi hotels. The dining programme is surprisingly broad: Sevilla for Mediterranean, Dhaba for robust Indian, and the charming Ye Olde Bakery. The central pool, framed by cabanas under palm trees, is one of the most pleasant hotel pools in Delhi — sociable rather than spectacular. The in-house pianist in the lobby is a detail that tells you exactly what kind of hotel this is. Aurangzeb Road is five minutes to Khan Market and ten to Lodi Garden, making this one of the best-located heritage hotels in the capital.
knownFor: 1956 colonial heritage architecture, three-acre garden estate, Ye Olde Bakery, Sevilla Mediterranean restaurant, Dhaba Indian restaurant, and the most gracious address in Lutyens' Diplomatic Quarter
address: 12, Dr. APJ Abdul Kalam Road (Aurangzeb Road), New Delhi, Delhi 110011
amenities: 4 Restaurants & Bars (Sevilla, Dhaba, Ye Olde Bakery, Piccadilly Bar)

name: The LaLiT New Delhi
tagline: The largest standard rooms at any Delhi five-star — walkable Connaught Place location and exceptional value at the luxury tier.
description: The LaLiT New Delhi is the secret that experienced Delhi travellers and well-travelled domestic guests often keep to themselves: a genuine five-star hotel with the largest standard guest rooms in the city, a central Connaught Place location within walking distance of Jantar Mantar and Gurudwara Bangla Sahib, and rates that consistently undercut the Oberoi, Leela, and Shangri-La by 30-40% for a comparable room-standard experience. The hotel has five restaurants, a bakery, spa, fitness centre, and swimming pool. The Baluchi restaurant is well regarded for North Indian cuisine. The LaLiT Group has built its reputation on attentive Indian hospitality and meticulous maintenance — the hotels are always spotless and the staff genuinely engaged. The art gallery in the lobby adds a cultural dimension that few Delhi hotels attempt. For first-timers to Delhi who want five-star standards, a central location, and the ability to walk to the major Connaught Place-area attractions, The LaLiT is the most intelligent booking in the city.
knownFor: Largest standard rooms at any Delhi five-star, walkable Connaught Place, Baluchi North Indian restaurant, strong value at the luxury tier, and exemplary Indian hospitality culture
address: Barakhamba Avenue, Connaught Place, New Delhi, Delhi 110001
amenities: 5 Restaurants & Bars (Baluchi, Rejuvenate Tea Lounge, 24/7, Kitty Su Nightclub, Piano Bar)

name: Maidens Hotel, New Delhi
tagline: Delhi's oldest operational hotel — 1903 colonial heritage in Civil Lines, where peacocks roam the lawns near Kashmiri Gate.
description: Maidens Hotel has been receiving guests since 1903, making it not merely Delhi's oldest continuously operating hotel but one of the oldest in South Asia. The pearl-white colonial facade, the sweeping verandahs, the high-ceilinged rooms, and the gardens where peacocks genuinely roam — Maidens is a time capsule that has been sensitively preserved rather than aggressively 'modernised'. Now managed by the Oberoi Group under the heritage Maidens Hotel brand, the property has 54 rooms and suites furnished with period furniture and equipped with thoughtful modern amenities. The hotel sits in Civil Lines — Delhi's administrative suburb north of Old Delhi — within walking distance of the Kashmiri Gate memorial, the Northern Railway headquarters, and the city's earliest British-era architecture. Guests who want to understand Delhi's oldest layer, and who value authenticity over luxury amenity counts, find Maidens incomparable. The restaurant and bar carry on the colonial tradition, and afternoon tea on the verandah remains one of Delhi's more distinguished genteel pleasures.
knownFor: Delhi's oldest hotel (1903), peacocks on the lawns, Oberoi-managed heritage experience, verandah afternoon tea, Civil Lines location near Old Delhi, and the most historically authentic hotel stay in the capital
address: 7, Sham Nath Marg, Civil Lines, New Delhi, Delhi 110054

name: The Manor, New Delhi
tagline: Delhi's original boutique hotel — a 1950s bungalow in Friends Colony with INJA fusion cuisine and a zen garden.
description: The Manor is the hotel that Delhi insiders have been recommending quietly to discerning visitors for twenty years. Built in the 1950s in Friends Colony West — one of South Delhi's most established and leafy residential enclaves — the property underwent a comprehensive renovation under international designers and relaunched as a genuinely contemporary boutique property without losing its soul. The hotel has only 14 rooms, set around nearly an acre of secluded garden, and the intimacy and privacy this creates is simply unavailable at any of Delhi's larger five-stars. The rooms are elegant in a minimalist way: king beds, 4K televisions, private terraces or garden views, with warm lighting and natural materials. The INJA restaurant — a Japanese-Indian fusion concept — has built a serious reputation among Delhi's food community. The roof terrace is one of the calmest places in Delhi for a drink at sunset. Friends Colony puts the hotel close to the Nizamuddin railway hub, Lodi Garden, and Khan Market. For guests who want to feel like they're staying in a private South Delhi home rather than a hotel, The Manor is unmatched.
knownFor: Delhi's oldest boutique hotel (1950s bungalow), INJA Japanese-Indian restaurant, Friends Colony private garden, 14-room exclusivity, zen atmosphere, and the most residential South Delhi experience available
address: 77, Friends Colony West, New Delhi, Delhi 110065

name: Haveli Dharampura
tagline: A 19th-century haveli in the gullies of Old Delhi — the only luxury stay inside the walled city, steps from Jama Masjid.
description: Haveli Dharampura is one of the most extraordinary hotel experiences available anywhere in Delhi — a meticulously restored 19th-century courtyard mansion deep inside Old Delhi's gullies, accessible only on foot through the lanes off Chatta Lal Mian, steps from the massive south gate of Jama Masjid. The property was a crumbling ruin when designer Vijay Goel acquired and restored it over nearly a decade into a 14-room boutique heritage property. The result is magnificent: soaring interior courtyards with colonnaded arches, hand-painted frescoes, jhali screens, and carved wooden balconies that look out over the rooftops of Shahjahanabad — the Mughal city that Shah Jahan built in the 17th century. The rooftop offers direct views of the Jama Masjid's minarets and dome, perhaps the most dramatic hotel rooftop view in India. Staying here means being woken by the azaan from the mosque, eating breakfast looking at the Red Fort, and walking into one of the densest and most alive urban environments on earth. It is not for every guest, but for the historically curious traveller, there is nowhere in Delhi — perhaps nowhere in India — quite like this.
knownFor: Only luxury hotel inside Old Delhi's walled city, Jama Masjid rooftop views, 19th-century haveli restoration, Shahjahanabad Mughal heritage experience, and the most dramatically located hotel in Delhi
address: 2213, Gali Guliyan, Chatta Lal Mian, Dharampura, Old Delhi, New Delhi, Delhi 110006

name: The Connaught, New Delhi (IHCL SeleQtions)
tagline: A Taj-curated boutique on Connaught Place — outdoor pool, heritage architecture, and the most central four-star address in Delhi.
description: The Connaught is part of IHCL's SeleQtions portfolio — a curated collection of non-Taj-branded hotels that carry the group's quality standards but their own distinct character. On Shaheed Bhagat Singh Marg, the hotel sits at the inner ring of Connaught Place itself, the geometric centre of New Delhi, within walking distance of the Rajiv Chowk metro, Jantar Mantar, and a dozen significant landmarks. The architecture retains the Art Deco character of the Connaught Place buildings. The outdoor pool is a genuine surprise — unexpected at a hotel this central — and the poolside lounge is a popular gathering point for Delhi's professional class. The rooms are thoughtfully done, not merely functional, and the service reflects IHCL's training culture. For guests who want to be in the absolute heart of Delhi without paying five-star rates, or who simply prefer a boutique property to a grand hotel, The Connaught is the correct booking — particularly given its Rajiv Chowk walkability.
knownFor: Connaught Place's own hotel, IHCL SeleQtions curation, outdoor pool at the centre of Delhi, Rajiv Chowk walkability, and the best-located four-star in the capital's commercial heart
address: 37, Shaheed Bhagat Singh Marg, Connaught Place, New Delhi, Delhi 110001

name: Radisson Blu Marina Hotel Connaught Place
tagline: Connaught Place's Radisson Blu — outdoor pool, multiple restaurants, and modern four-star comfort steps from India's commercial centre.
description: Radisson Blu Marina Connaught Place delivers the consistent Radisson Blu standard — reliable quality, proper amenities, and a loyal corporate following — in one of Delhi's most coveted hotel addresses. The Connaught Place location places guests a short walk from the central metro interchange, the Connaught Place shopping and dining circuit, Jantar Mantar, and the axis roads to India Gate and the government district. The hotel has an outdoor pool, a well-equipped gym, and three dining options including the popular Open House café. The rooms are modern and well-maintained, with the design sensibility of an international mid-luxury brand. Business travellers repeat-book because the property is dependable, central, and reasonably priced relative to the five-star competition on the same streets. The meeting room infrastructure is solid for SME corporate events.
knownFor: Connaught Place central location, outdoor pool, Open House café, dependable corporate four-star standards, and one of the most consistently reviewed Radisson properties in India
address: B-19, Connaught Place, New Delhi, Delhi 110001
amenities: 3 Restaurants & Bars (Open House, Latitude, Marina Bar)

name: Lemon Tree Premier, Delhi Airport
tagline: Aerocity's most accessible premium property — 10-minute walk from Terminal 3, pool, and three restaurants at a sensible rate.
description: Lemon Tree Premier Delhi Airport is the best-value hotel in the Aerocity hospitality district — a property that offers a genuine four-star product at rates meaningfully below the JW Marriott and Pullman that surround it. The location is unbeatable for airport transit: it is a 10-minute walk through the covered Aerocity metro walkway to Terminal 3's departures. The rooms are modern, spacious for the price point, and well-equipped for business travellers. The hotel has three restaurants including Citrus (Mediterranean with an outdoor terrace), Slounge (the rooftop bar), and Republic of Noodles for South-East Asian. The pool is compact but functional. For domestic travellers who want four-star standards at the airport without paying the JW premium, or international guests with an early-morning connection out of Delhi, Lemon Tree Premier is the most rational choice in Aerocity.
knownFor: 10-minute walk to IGI Terminal 3, Slounge rooftop bar, best value four-star in Aerocity, Republic of Noodles restaurant, and the most honest price-to-quality ratio near Delhi airport
address: Asset Area 6, Hospitality District, Aerocity, New Delhi, Delhi 110037
amenities: 3 Restaurants & Bars (Citrus, Slounge Rooftop, Republic of Noodles)

name: The Umrao
tagline: Aerocity's most design-forward property — bold interiors, strong F&B, and four-star sophistication near the airport.
description: The Umrao is Aerocity's boutique contrarian — a hotel that decided to compete on design and personality rather than scale and brand recognition in a district otherwise dominated by global hotel chains. The interiors are genuinely ambitious: the lobby features dramatic installations, the rooms carry a strong visual identity, and the bar and restaurant spaces are clearly designed for people who notice things. Dhilli — the hotel's Indian restaurant — has built a solid local following for its regionally diverse menu and atmospheric setting. The hotel's position in Aerocity provides the same airport proximity as the JW Marriott and Lemon Tree Premier, with a distinctly more individual character. Business travellers who find the sameness of large-brand airport hotels wearing will find The Umrao a worthwhile alternative. The pool area is particularly well done.
knownFor: Aerocity's most characterful boutique hotel, Dhilli Indian restaurant, bold design-forward interiors, strong F&B culture, and the best personality among Aerocity's four-star options
address: Asset Area 4, Hospitality District, Aerocity, New Delhi, Delhi 110037

name: Bloom Hotel - Connaught Place
tagline: The new benchmark for smart three-star stays in Delhi — design-led rooms, free breakfast, and Connaught Place on your doorstep.
description: Bloom Hotels are the most intelligently conceived mid-range hotel brand in India today, and the Connaught Place location is among their flagship properties. The brand's formula is specific: design-led rooms (typically smaller than traditional hotels but impeccably fitted out), complimentary breakfast for all guests, high-speed WiFi, and a complete avoidance of the drabness that afflicts most Indian three-star properties. The Connaught Place location puts guests within a 15-minute walk of virtually every central Delhi attraction — India Gate, Jantar Mantar, Gurudwara Bangla Sahib, National Museum, and the Rajiv Chowk metro. The staff are notably well-trained for the price point. For budget-conscious travellers who refuse to compromise on aesthetics, or for solo travellers and young couples who want central Delhi at a sensible price, Bloom Connaught Place is the go-to recommendation.
knownFor: Delhi's best smart three-star brand, complimentary breakfast, design-led rooms, Connaught Place walkability, Rajiv Chowk metro access, and the strongest value proposition in central Delhi
address: K-11, Connaught Place, New Delhi, Delhi 110001

name: The Hans Hotel
tagline: A Connaught Place institution — the Hans has been a reliable, well-located three-star near Janpath since 1959.
description: The Hans Hotel has been part of Connaught Place's hotel landscape since 1959 and occupies a solid place in the city's mid-range hierarchy. The hotel is not flashy, but it is consistent, clean, and well-priced for its location — on Kasturba Gandhi Marg, within easy walking distance of Janpath, Connaught Place's inner circle, India Gate, and the National Museum. The rooms are larger than the Bloom standard and the dining options — including the long-running 24-hour Grill Room — are more substantial. Business travellers who want a familiar, no-surprises base in central Delhi, and who prefer traditional hotel character over boutique aesthetics, return to The Hans repeatedly. The property has been refurbished periodically and the current standard is well-maintained. The 1959 vintage means the building has genuine ceiling heights and room proportions that modern hotels in the same segment rarely achieve.
knownFor: Connaught Place heritage since 1959, large legacy room sizes, Grill Room 24-hour dining, Kasturba Gandhi Marg location near India Gate, and the most established mid-range address in central Delhi
address: 15, Kasturba Gandhi Marg, New Delhi, Delhi 110001

name: Jaypee Siddharth
tagline: Rajendra Place's Jaypee four-star — pool, multi-cuisine dining, and solid business infrastructure between Connaught Place and the Ring Road.
description: Jaypee Siddharth is the Jaypee Group's mid-Delhi property, situated in Rajendra Place — a commercial area roughly equidistant between Connaught Place to the south and the AIIMS-Green Park corridor to the north. The hotel has 101 rooms and suites, a swimming pool, a gym, a well-regarded multi-cuisine restaurant (Latitude), a café, and a bar. It is a dependable, corporate-oriented four-star that attracts a largely domestic business clientele and families visiting Delhi for medical appointments at AIIMS or Safdarjung Hospital. The pool is well-maintained and the gym is properly equipped. For guests whose primary Delhi commitment is in the Rajendra Place, Karol Bagh, or Patel Nagar corridor, Jaypee Siddharth is the most sensible hotel in that zone — more comfortable than the Paharganj options and better-located than Aerocity.
knownFor: Rajendra Place location between CP and AIIMS, outdoor pool, Latitude multi-cuisine restaurant, solid Jaypee Group corporate four-star standards, and strong value for medical-district visitors
address: 3, Rajendra Place, New Delhi, Delhi 110008

name: Hotel Palace Heights
tagline: Connaught Place's best-kept secret — a clean, central three-star with excellent breakfast and unbeatable metro access.
description: Hotel Palace Heights holds a cult status among experienced independent travellers who discover it: a clean, well-run three-star property in the middle ring of Connaught Place, within a two-minute walk of the Rajiv Chowk metro interchange that connects to virtually every part of Delhi, and within easy walking distance of India Gate, Janpath, and the area's restaurants and shopping. The rooms are not large, but they are impeccably maintained. The breakfast — included in the rate — is notably good for the price point and receives consistent mention in reviews. The rooftop is a genuine bonus. Loyal guests include solo international backpackers who have moved up from budget hostels and mid-range domestic travellers who know the value of this location. For the budget-to-mid-range traveller who wants to be properly central in Delhi without paying boutique or luxury rates, Palace Heights consistently delivers.
knownFor: Connaught Place inner ring location, Rajiv Chowk metro (2-minute walk), included breakfast, rooftop access, and the highest reviews-per-rupee ratio of any three-star in central Delhi
address: D Block, 26-28, Connaught Place, New Delhi, Delhi 110001

name: Vivanta New Delhi, Dwarka
tagline: A Taj Vivanta in Dwarka — the most polished hotel in West Delhi, ideal for Dwarka residents and IGI Airport connectivity.
description: Vivanta New Delhi Dwarka brings the Taj Group's Vivanta brand — the more accessible, contemporary sister to the flagship Taj properties — to Dwarka, the sprawling West Delhi residential suburb that is home to over a million residents and sits 15 minutes from IGI Airport. The hotel is the best hotel in the entire West Delhi corridor, a fact that gives it a natural market among Dwarka's professional community, those visiting relatives there, and travellers who want airport proximity without Aerocity pricing. The rooms are contemporary, the facilities complete (pool, spa, gym), and the dining programme — including the Sky High rooftop restaurant — is well-regarded for the area. The Vivanta brand's service philosophy is notably warmer and less formal than the Taj flagship hotels, which many guests find refreshing.
knownFor: Best hotel in West Delhi by a wide margin, Sky High rooftop restaurant, Taj Vivanta service culture, Dwarka's professional community base, and the most practical option between Aerocity and central Delhi for West Delhi visitors
address: Plot No. 4, Sector 21, Dwarka, New Delhi, Delhi 110075
amenities: 3 Restaurants & Bars (Sky High Rooftop, Latitude, Bar)

name: Le Méridien New Delhi
tagline: Windsor Place's art-driven five-star — where contemporary design and a Connaught Place address meet Marriott standards.
description: Le Méridien New Delhi sits on Windsor Place, a stone's throw from Janpath and the Connaught Place inner circle, and is the most design-conscious of the international chain five-stars in the CP zone. The brand's global 'Unlock Art' philosophy manifests here in an active art programme — rotating exhibitions, curated installations, and an aesthetic sensibility that permeates even the rooms. The 358 rooms are generously proportioned with good natural light. Le Méridien's renowned breakfast — considered among the best hotel breakfasts in Delhi — has been drawing walk-in guests for years. The rooftop MeSa restaurant and bar commands excellent views over the Lutyens skyline. The location is near-unbeatable for exploration on foot or by metro. For guests who want a proper international luxury brand at competitive rates with a creative character, Le Méridien sits at the right point on the CP-zone spectrum.
knownFor: Windsor Place location, 'Unlock Art' programme with rotating exhibitions, best hotel breakfast in Delhi, MeSa rooftop restaurant and bar, and strong Marriott programme value near Connaught Place
address: Windsor Place, New Delhi, Delhi 110001
amenities: 4 Restaurants & Bars (MeSa Rooftop, Le Belvedere, Latest Recipe, Longitude Bar)

name: Crowne Plaza New Delhi Rohini
tagline: The IHG five-star in North-West Delhi — serving Rohini's booming residential and corporate corridor with full Crowne Plaza standards.
description: Crowne Plaza New Delhi Rohini is the best hotel in North-West Delhi — a complete five-star property that serves the large residential and commercial community of Rohini, Pitampura, and the outer northwest ring. It fills a genuine hospitality gap in a part of Delhi that is home to hundreds of thousands of residents but was previously underserved by quality accommodation. The hotel has an outdoor pool, a full spa, gym, multiple dining outlets including a rooftop, and the complete IHG Crowne Plaza service infrastructure. Business travellers from the area's commercial zones and families hosting events in Rohini form the core clientele. Access to Rohini Metro stations makes the property functional for city-wide travel. For guests whose appointments, family, or events are in North Delhi, this is the unambiguous choice.
knownFor: North-West Delhi's best hotel, full Crowne Plaza standards in an underserved corridor, rooftop dining, metro access, and the most complete hospitality offering in the Rohini-Pitampura zone
address: Plot No. 9, Community Centre, Rohini, Sector-10, New Delhi, Delhi 110085

name: Ibis New Delhi Aerocity
tagline: Aerocity's most reliable budget-smart option — Accor standards, easy airport access, and honest pricing in the airport district.
description: Ibis New Delhi Aerocity does one thing and does it well: it provides consistent, clean, functional accommodation in the Aerocity hospitality district at a price that is about half the JW Marriott and 30% below the Lemon Tree Premier. The Accor Ibis brand's global consistency means the experience is highly predictable — modern rooms that are small but thoughtfully laid out, strong WiFi, the signature Ibis Kitchen with simple but good food, and helpful 24-hour staff. The airport connectivity is identical to the five-stars next door: the Aerocity metro station and the covered walkways to Terminal 3 are shared. For frequent flyers, corporate travellers on a managed travel budget, or anyone wanting an overnight stay near the airport without paying luxury rates, Ibis Aerocity is the most rational booking.
knownFor: Most price-competitive airport hotel in Delhi, Accor Ibis reliability, Aerocity metro walkability, Ibis Kitchen round-the-clock dining, and the best budget-smart airport stay in India
address: Asset 5, Hospitality District, Aerocity, New Delhi, Delhi 110037

name: WelcomHotel New Delhi (ITC)
tagline: ITC's Saket five-star — close to Select Citywalk and DLF, with the full WelcomHotel culinary tradition in South Delhi.
description: WelcomHotel New Delhi is ITC's five-star property in the Saket-South Delhi corridor, positioned to serve the commercial and residential fabric of South Delhi's most prosperous neighbourhoods. It sits close to Select Citywalk, one of Delhi's most popular malls, and the DLF South Court shopping zone. The hotel carries ITC's WelcomHotel brand's trademark strengths: exceptional food — the Dum Pukht at WelcomHotel brings the same Awadhi culinary tradition found at ITC Maurya — a high standard of room finish, and the group's ecological commitment. Guests who are based in South Delhi for social or professional reasons find WelcomHotel the most convenient five-star, combining the brand's quality standards with a significantly shorter commute than the Chanakyapuri cluster.
knownFor: ITC WelcomHotel brand quality in South Delhi, Dum Pukht Awadhi cuisine, proximity to Select Citywalk mall, ecological commitment, and the finest five-star address serving the Saket-GK corridor
address: District Centre, Saket, New Delhi, Delhi 110017
amenities: 3 Restaurants & Bars (Dum Pukht, Ottimo, ITC One Lounge)

name: Park Inn by Radisson New Delhi IP Extension
tagline: East Delhi's most dependable three-star — clean, connected by metro, and well-priced in the IP Extension commercial zone.
description: Park Inn by Radisson IP Extension is a well-executed three-star property in East Delhi's IP Extension area — one of the city's older planned commercial districts, near the Patparganj industrial zone and a short metro ride from Pragati Maidan and the city centre. The Radisson Park Inn brand is built on honest, consistent delivery: clean rooms, reliable WiFi, a functional restaurant, and professional service without pretension. The hotel serves the East Delhi corporate and residential community that often lacks quality hotel options in their vicinity. Guests visiting businesses or family in East Delhi, or attending events at Pragati Maidan, find this property a sensible, well-priced base with predictable standards.
knownFor: Best hotel in East Delhi, Radisson Park Inn reliability, IP Extension location near Patparganj, metro connectivity to city centre, and strong value for East Delhi business visitors
address: Plot 26, IP Extension, Patparganj, New Delhi, Delhi 110092

name: Aloft New Delhi Aerocity
tagline: Aerocity's most social hotel — Marriott's millennial-forward brand with a lively WXYZ bar and design-forward rooms.
description: Aloft New Delhi Aerocity brings Marriott's millennial-forward Aloft brand to the airport district, and the result is the most lively and sociable hotel in Aerocity. The WXYZ Bar is the best bar in the hospitality district — a social hub that draws both hotel guests and local professionals. The rooms carry the Aloft signature: vibrant colours, smart storage, rain showers, and an energetic design aesthetic that is a deliberate counterpoint to the corporate blandness of most airport hotels. The Re:mix lounge, Re:charge gym, and Re:fuel market round out the brand's experiential programme. The hotel is connected to the airport metro and within easy reach of the terminal. For younger travellers, creatives, and those who find the Marriott and Pullman formats too stiff, Aloft is the Aerocity answer.
knownFor: Aerocity's most social and design-forward hotel, WXYZ Bar (best bar in the district), Aloft Marriott brand energy, Re:mix lounge, and the most energetic atmosphere near IGI airport
address: Asset Area 4, Hospitality District, Aerocity, New Delhi, Delhi 110037

name: The Suryaa New Delhi
tagline: New Friends Colony's understated five-star — home to Threesixtyone° and an extensive art collection, near the Nizamuddin hub.
description: The Suryaa New Delhi is South-East Delhi's landmark luxury hotel — a five-star property in New Friends Colony that serves the residential fabric of South Delhi and East Delhi while offering an unusually strong arts and culture identity for its category. The hotel has an extensive collection of Indian contemporary art displayed throughout its public spaces. The restaurant Threesixtyone° is one of the better all-day fine-dining venues in South Delhi, with a diverse international spread. The Suryaa's proximity to the Nizamuddin railway station and the Hazrat Nizamuddin Dargah makes it a natural base for guests arriving by train and for those interested in Sufi heritage. The hotel's corporate following is large — it sits at the intersection of several key commercial zones — and the meeting infrastructure is well-developed. An outdoor pool, a full spa, and a gym complete the amenity picture.
knownFor: Indian contemporary art collection, Threesixtyone° all-day restaurant, New Friends Colony location near Nizamuddin, access to Sufi heritage, and the most culturally engaged five-star in South-East Delhi
address: New Friends Colony, New Delhi, Delhi 110025

name: Bloomrooms @ New Delhi Railway Station
tagline: Paharganj's most design-led smart hotel — 2 minutes from New Delhi Station, Bloom quality at the city's primary rail gateway.
description: Bloomrooms at New Delhi Railway Station is the Bloom brand's strategically placed property at the most important transit hub in northern India — New Delhi Railway Station is the second-busiest railway station in the world by passenger volume, and the hotel sits within a 2-minute walk. For travellers arriving and departing by train who want a smart, clean, design-forward room without the grimness of most Paharganj budget options, this Bloom property is a revelation at the price. The rooms carry the brand's signature — compact but impeccably fitted, with design details that make the space feel curated rather than budget. The complimentary breakfast is included. The location means every part of Delhi is accessible by auto, metro (New Delhi Station), or the Airport Express Line at Shivaji Stadium. It is the obvious answer to the question 'where do I stay in Delhi if I'm arriving and leaving by train?'
knownFor: 2-minute walk to New Delhi Railway Station, Bloom brand design quality, complimentary breakfast, Airport Express Line access, and the smartest budget-to-mid hotel at Delhi's primary rail gateway
address: Paharganj, Opposite New Delhi Railway Station, New Delhi, Delhi 110055

name: The Metropolitan Hotel & Spa
tagline: Bangla Sahib Road's Japanese-zen five-star — the most minimalist luxury hotel in Delhi, two minutes from Connaught Place.
description: The Metropolitan Hotel & Spa is Delhi's most restrained and design-conscious five-star — a Japanese-influenced property on Bangla Sahib Road that sits within two minutes of Connaught Place yet feels like an entirely separate world. The hotel was the first Nikko-affiliated property in India and the aesthetic that results is markedly different from the ornate grandeur of competing five-stars: clean lines, natural materials, muted colours, and an emphasis on calm. Sakura — the hotel's Japanese restaurant — remains one of the best Japanese dining options in Delhi. The Aura spa is designed on Zen principles. The 178 rooms are quietly luxurious rather than ostentatious. For guests who find the Taj and Leela aesthetic overwhelming, or who appreciate Japanese minimalism as a counterpoint to the city's sensory intensity, The Metropolitan is a deeply satisfying choice — and the Bangla Sahib location is among the best in the city for access to CP, India Gate, and public transport.
knownFor: Delhi's only Japanese-influenced luxury hotel, Sakura Japanese restaurant, Aura Zen spa, Bangla Sahib Road walkability to CP, and the most minimalist and restful five-star experience in the capital
address: Bangla Sahib Road, Behind Connaught Place, New Delhi, Delhi 110001
amenities: 3 Restaurants & Bars (Sakura Japanese, Cilantro, Lobby Lounge)

name: Bloom Hotel - Karol Bagh
tagline: Karol Bagh's smartest mid-range stay — Bloom design quality and free breakfast in Delhi's busiest shopping district.
description: Bloom Hotel Karol Bagh brings the brand's winning formula — compact but impeccably designed rooms, complimentary breakfast, high-speed WiFi, and spotless maintenance — to one of Delhi's most commercially alive neighbourhoods. Karol Bagh is the city's mid-market shopping heartland: a dense grid of electronics markets, textile shops, gold jewellers, and restaurants that draws buyers from across North India. The hotel sits within easy walking distance of the Karol Bagh metro station on the Blue Line, which connects the area to Rajiv Chowk in ten minutes. Guests visiting for wholesale purchasing, family functions in West Delhi, or simply wanting a design-led base at a fair price find the Bloom Karol Bagh an excellent proposition. The breakfast quality and WiFi reliability consistently earn top marks.
knownFor: Bloom brand design quality in Karol Bagh, complimentary breakfast, Blue Line metro access, proximity to Karol Bagh wholesale markets, and the best smart-budget option in West-Central Delhi
address: 14, Arya Samaj Road, Karol Bagh, New Delhi, Delhi 110005

name: Hotel Smyle Inn
tagline: Paharganj's most civilised three-star — a clean, reliable base for international travellers near New Delhi Station.
